title: Bryantiella glutinosa
intro: Bryantiella glutinosa, also known as Sida rhombifolia, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of conditions like fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Bryantiella glutinosa include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in various commercial herbal products such as capsules, teas, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Bryantiella glutinosa is classified within the family Collemataceae. This classification places it in the suborder Plicariinae. The species falls under the genus Bryantiella. It belongs to the phylum Ascomycota. This classification is based on its characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the fungal kingdom.
